content.problemStatement:
A single car can accommodate at most 444 people.

NNN friends want to go to a restaurant for a party. Find the **minimum** number of cars required to accommodate all the friends.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains an integer NNN - denoting the number of friends.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum number of cars required to accommodate all the friends.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the number of friends for that test case.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line – the minimum number of cars required.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Each car holds 4 people.
- N=4 → 1 car.
- N=2 → 1 car.
- N=7 → ceil(7/4)=2 cars.
- N=98 → ceil(98/4)=25 cars.
N=2 → 1 car, N=5 → ceil(5/4)=2 cars, N=12 → ceil(12/4)=3 cars.

solution.approach: Each car can hold at most 4 people. The answer for a given N is the ceiling of N/4, which can be computed with integer arithmetic as (N + 3) / 4. Process each test case independently.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
